decision: Keep Tailscale as the private transport and enforce actor identity, account scope, least-privilege thread actions, runtime expiry, and bounded secret-free authorization receipts in the shared Hermes protocol server.

sources.finding: Use least-privilege access controls, tests, audit logs, and removal of unused clients and keys.
OAuth access is scoped and access tokens expire, reinforcing scoped and expiring Hermes grants.
Checked for July 2026 product changes before finalizing the transport decision.
Use normalized HTTP attributes and avoid sensitive header capture.
Keep a vendor-neutral callback because agent semantic conventions are still evolving.

rejected: New access proxy or identity vendor for the first policy increment
Authorization implemented separately in each client
Bearer tokens, headers, request bodies, or chat content in authorization receipts
